lens: Risk-off snap flips yesterday's rally (Nasdaq -1.9%, S&P -1.1%, VIX ~+20% back above 20), but signings keep coming: Blackstone's ~$7B sale of Aypa Power to Brookfield (BofA), Platinum Equity's $5.6B Peranel JV with Nestlé (BofA), Altaris' $18.50/share take-private of Simulations Plus out of a Morgan Stanley auction, and Nordic Capital's sale of ArisGlobal to Dassault (Evercore, Jefferies) — deals that still must grind through diligence to close regardless of the tape.

stories.headline: BofA advises on Brookfield's ~$7B acquisition of battery-storage platform Aypa Power from Blackstone
stories.why_it_matters: Brookfield agreed to acquire Aypa Power, a leading North American battery-storage platform (~6.5 GW operating, 20 GW+ pipeline), from Blackstone at a ~$7B enterprise value. The release names Cantor Fitzgerald as lead adviser with BofA Securities also advising the seller side. A large sponsor-to-sponsor energy-infrastructure exit runs heavy asset, contract, and financing diligence.
stories.suggested_action: Engage BofA's power & infrastructure coverage on the Aypa exit — a $7B sponsor-to-sponsor platform sale runs document-intensive asset and offtake diligence across many parties.

deal_clock.event: Brookfield agrees to acquire Aypa Power from Blackstone (~$7B EV)

stories.headline: BofA advises Platinum Equity on its $5.6B Peranel water & premium-beverages JV with Nestlé
stories.why_it_matters: Platinum Equity and Nestlé agreed to create Peranel, a $5.6B (EUR 4.9B) water and premium-beverages leader, as a 50/50 joint venture carved out of Nestlé Waters. The release names BofA Securities as financial adviser to Platinum Equity (buyer). A large carve-out JV runs complex separation, TSA, and cross-border diligence into a 1H-2027 close.
stories.suggested_action: Open with BofA's consumer/sponsors coverage on Peranel — carve-out JVs run heavy separation and TSA diligence across shared rooms through a long close.

deal_clock.event: Platinum Equity + Nestlé agree to create Peranel ($5.6B EV)

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley advises Simulations Plus on its $18.50/share, ~$375M take-private by Altaris
stories.why_it_matters: Simulations Plus (SLP) agreed to be taken private by Altaris at $18.50/share in cash (~$375M, ~26% premium). The definitive proxy (DEFM14A) shows Morgan Stanley ran a formal auction — contacting 23 parties, 16 diligence processes — and delivered the fairness opinion to SLP's board (seller). A completed auction now advancing to the shareholder vote.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Morgan Stanley's healthcare/tech coverage on Simulations Plus — a sponsor take-private out of a full auction runs concentrated confirmatory diligence into the vote.

deal_clock.event: SLP files DEFM14A; $18.50/share cash from Altaris

stories.headline: Lazard and Houlihan Lokey advise LivePerson as its SoundHound AI stock merger heads to an Aug 20 vote
stories.why_it_matters: A fresh Jul 23 proxy solicitation for SoundHound AI's acquisition of LivePerson (LPSN) confirms Lazard ran LivePerson's strategic-review/sale process and Houlihan Lokey delivered the fairness opinion (both target-side). Most holders receive SoundHound stock (~$3.33/share at announcement, ~22% premium) with a collar; a distressed-balance-sheet deal (secured noteholders taking a discount) advancing to a special meeting.
stories.suggested_action: A special-meeting run-in is a clean reason to engage Lazard and Houlihan Lokey on secure, auditable diligence for a stock merger with a noteholder workstream.

deal_clock.event: SoundHound/LivePerson merger announced
LivePerson special meeting / shareholder vote

stories.headline: Citi advises Progress Software on its $400M acquisition of Domo's AI & data platform; Jefferies advises Domo
stories.why_it_matters: Progress Software agreed to acquire Domo's AI and data-platform business for $400M in cash. The release names Citi as exclusive adviser to Progress (buyer) and Jefferies as exclusive adviser to Domo (seller). A carve-out asset purchase funded via cash and revolver runs two-sided diligence into a close by Progress's Nov-30 fiscal year-end.
stories.suggested_action: Open with Citi's software coverage on Progress/Domo — an asset carve-out runs focused IP, data, and separation diligence on a tight fiscal-year timeline.

deal_clock.event: Progress agrees to acquire Domo's AI/data platform for $400M

stories.headline: Evercore and Jefferies advise Nordic Capital on its sale of ArisGlobal to Dassault Systèmes
stories.why_it_matters: Nordic Capital agreed to sell ArisGlobal, an AI-enabled life-sciences software platform (~$175M 2026 revenue), to Dassault Systèmes. The release names Evercore and Jefferies as advisers to the target/seller ArisGlobal. A sponsor software exit runs controlled, document-heavy diligence into a 2H-2026 close subject to regulatory clearance.
stories.suggested_action: Engage Evercore's (and Jefferies's) tech/sponsors coverage on the ArisGlobal sale — a PE software exit is a strong secure sell-side diligence use case.

deal_clock.event: Nordic Capital agrees to sell ArisGlobal to Dassault Systèmes

stories.headline: RBC (Richardson Barr) advises the seller on Matador's $1.275B Paloma Permian acquisition
stories.why_it_matters: Matador Resources agreed to acquire Paloma Permian (an EnCap portfolio company) for $1.275B in cash, adding ~16,235 net Delaware Basin acres. The release names RBC Richardson Barr as adviser to the seller. A sizable upstream A&D deal runs reserves, title, and lender diligence, funded with cash and RBL borrowings into a Q4-2026 close.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to RBC's energy A&D coverage on Matador/Paloma — upstream acreage deals run heavy reserves and title diligence a data room is built for.

deal_clock.event: Matador agrees to acquire Paloma Permian for $1.275B

stories.headline: Goldman and Wells Fargo advise Public Storage on its closed all-stock acquisition of National Storage Affiliates; Morgan Stanley advised NSA
stories.why_it_matters: Public Storage closed its all-stock acquisition of National Storage Affiliates Trust (0.14 PS share per NSA share), backed by ~$2.0B secured mortgage plus $237M mezzanine JV financing. The release names Goldman Sachs and Wells Fargo advising Public Storage (buyer, and providing the financing) and Morgan Stanley advising NSA (target). A completed public-REIT combination with financing and JV workstreams.
stories.suggested_action: Use the closed Public Storage/NSA deal as a post-close hook with Goldman's real estate coverage — integration and financing workstreams keep secure rooms active past signing.

deal_clock.event: Public Storage closes NSA acquisition

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley advises First Financial Bancorp on its ~$208M all-stock acquisition of Finward Bancorp; Stephens advises Finward
stories.why_it_matters: First Financial Bancorp agreed to acquire Finward Bancorp in an all-stock deal (~$208M, 1.35 FFBC shares per Finward share). The Form 425 names Morgan Stanley as adviser to First Financial (buyer) and Stephens as adviser to Finward (target), which rendered the fairness opinion. A community-bank combination advancing to an S-4 and shareholder vote into a Q4-2026 close.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Morgan Stanley's depositories/FIG coverage on First Financial/Finward — a signed bank merger runs two-sided diligence into the S-4 and vote.

deal_clock.event: First Financial agrees to acquire Finward Bancorp (~$208M)

stories.headline: Evercore advises Vita Coco on its $175M acquisition of coconut-supply firm Copra
stories.why_it_matters: The Vita Coco Company agreed to acquire Copra, Inc. for $175M upfront (80% cash / 20% stock) plus a $45M-$100M earnout payable in 2029. The release names Evercore as adviser to Vita Coco (buyer). A vertical-integration acquisition that closed at announcement — a smaller but clean strategic-buyer diligence engagement.
stories.suggested_action: A quick, closed strategic acquisition is a light-touch reason to stay in front of Evercore's consumer coverage on repeatable room setup for bolt-ons.

deal_clock.event: Vita Coco acquires Copra for $175M (closed)

stories.headline: Stifel's KBW runs Five Star Bancorp's ~$113.6M common-stock offering; Stephens and Raymond James co-manage
stories.why_it_matters: Five Star Bancorp (FSBC) priced a common-stock offering of 2,725,000 shares at $44.00 (~$113.6M net, plus an option). The release names Keefe, Bruyette & Woods (A Stifel Company) as sole bookrunner, with Stephens, D.A. Davidson, Raymond James and Brean Capital as co-managers. A bank-equity raise closing ~Jul 24 for growth and general corporate purposes.
stories.suggested_action: Use the Five Star raise as an ECM hook with Stifel/KBW's depositories desk — bank follow-ons run coordinated underwriting and closing diligence.

deal_clock.event: Five Star prices ~$113.6M offering at $44.00
Expected closing

stories.headline: Citi is sole bookrunner on B&R Technology Merger Corp.'s $325M SPAC IPO
stories.why_it_matters: B&R Technology Merger Corp., an AI-focused SPAC, completed a $325M IPO (32,500,000 units at $10.00). The release names Citigroup as sole book-running manager, with $325M held in trust and a 45-day over-allotment option. A sizable SPAC listing that will drive de-SPAC target diligence once a combination is identified.
stories.suggested_action: Note Citi's sole-bookrunner SPAC IPO — the eventual de-SPAC target search is a downstream diligence conversation worth seeding early.

deal_clock.event: B&R Technology Merger Corp. completes $325M IPO

stories.headline: Risk-off snap: Nasdaq -1.9%, S&P -1.1%, VIX spikes ~20% back above 20
stories.why_it_matters: US equities reversed sharply intraday Jul 23 — Nasdaq -1.9%, S&P -1.1%, Dow -0.9%, Russell 2000 -0.7% — with the VIX jumping ~20% back above 20 and the 10Y yield firming to ~4.71%. Advisory names sold off with the tape (GS -2.1%, MS -1.8%). Volatility resets are exactly when signed processes (Simulations Plus, ArisGlobal, Peranel) still need to grind through diligence to close.
stories.suggested_action: Frame outreach around a volatility reset — signed deals still have to close regardless of the tape, and room readiness is what keeps diligence on schedule.

opportunities.headline: Sell-side adviser to Simulations Plus on its $18.50/share, ~$375M take-private by Altaris
opportunities.why_it_matters: Morgan Stanley ran the formal auction and delivered the fairness opinion to Simulations Plus on its signed $18.50/share (~$375M) take-private by Altaris. A completed sponsor auction now runs concentrated confirmatory diligence through the shareholder vote.

deal_clock.event: SLP files DEFM14A; $18.50/share cash from Altaris

opportunities.outreach_idea: Reach out to Morgan Stanley's healthcare/tech coverage on Simulations Plus — a sponsor take-private out of a full auction runs concentrated confirmatory diligence into the vote.

Congratulations on Morgan Stanley's role advising Simulations Plus on its take-private by Altaris. opportunities.headline: Adviser (with Leerink) to Crinetics on its $85/share, all-cash sale to Vertex
opportunities.why_it_matters: J.P. Morgan and Leerink advise Crinetics on its signed $85.00/share all-cash sale to Vertex (Lazard and Morgan Stanley advise Vertex), with a three-bank debt commitment. A sizable, financed biopharma take-private advancing to the shareholder vote.

deal_clock.event: Crinetics files preliminary proxy; $85.00/share cash from Vertex

market_snapshot.macro_note: Risk-off reversal on Jul 23: S&P -1.1% (7,420), Nasdaq -1.9% (25,197), Dow -0.9%, Russell 2000 -0.7%, with the VIX spiking ~20% back above 20 and the 10Y firming to ~4.71%. Advisory names sold off (GS -2.1%, MS -1.8%, JPM roughly flat). Values are LIVE INTRADAY levels captured midday EDT Jul 23 (market open) — not a settled close. EVR omitted (Yahoo returned a stale prior-close snapshot) and WTI omitted — accuracy over completeness.

talking_point.quote: The tape flipped risk-off today — VIX back above 20, Nasdaq down nearly 2% — but the signings didn't stop: Brookfield buying Aypa Power at ~$7B, Platinum Equity's $5.6B Peranel JV with Nestlé, Altaris taking Simulations Plus private, Nordic Capital selling ArisGlobal to Dassault. Every one of these still has to close, and volatility is exactly when diligence timelines slip. How are you thinking about room readiness on the processes you're carrying into a choppier market?
talking_point.use_with: M&A, sponsors, energy, consumer, and software coverage teams (esp. BofA, Morgan Stanley, Citi, Evercore, RBC)
talking_point.context: Pairs a Jul 23 risk-off reversal with a dense signing tape — a natural way to open a room-readiness conversation as deals push through a more volatile close.

ranked.read: Blackstone sells battery-storage platform Aypa Power to Brookfield at ~$7B (BofA sell-side); Matador buys Paloma Permian for $1.275B (RBC Richardson Barr sell-side).

Progress buys Domo's AI/data platform for $400M (Citi; Jefferies), Nordic Capital sells ArisGlobal to Dassault (Evercore, Jefferies), and SoundHound's LivePerson stock merger heads to an Aug 20 vote (Lazard, Houlihan Lokey).
ranked.source_note: PR Newswire + SEC EDGAR + StockTitan, Jul 22-23 2026

ranked.read: Platinum Equity + Nestlé form the $5.6B Peranel water & premium-beverages JV (BofA buy-side); Vita Coco buys Copra for $175M (Evercore).
First Financial acquires Finward Bancorp (~$208M; Morgan Stanley, Stephens); Five Star Bancorp raises ~$113.6M (KBW/Stifel bookrunner).

pipeline_watch.note: Named covered-bank mandates and sector heat. Rumored / no-adviser situations live in the Rumor Mill.

skeptics_corner.note: Unconfirmed leads — rumored deals and situations with no adviser named yet (or advisers unconfirmed). Real reporting, but treat as speculative: chase them, don't quote them on a call.

items.lead: The Dutch engineering/consultancy group (~EUR 3.2B market cap) has reportedly attracted takeover interest, including from Canadian peer WSP Global and several large-cap PE firms; shares jumped ~9.5% and were briefly halted.
items.why_unverified: Reuters 'sources' story; WSP said it does not comment on speculation and Arcadis declined to comment. No formal process or adviser confirmed, and the ~18% employee-led Lovinklaan foundation could block a deal.

items.lead: ACI is reportedly exploring a sale of its Biller/billing division, potentially valued around $1.5B (10-12x 2025 EBITDA), with early-stage talks said to include private equity firms.
items.why_unverified: Reuters 'three sources familiar'; ACI had not responded to comment requests and has not confirmed. No bank publicly named.

items.lead: Quadient's board formally announced a strategic review of its Lockers Solution business (28,000+ parcel lockers, EUR 114M FY25 revenue), weighing continued ownership, partnerships, geographic sales, or a full disposal.
items.why_unverified: Company-announced review with 'no certainty as to outcome, structure or timing'; no buyer identified and no transaction agreed. Next update expected with H1 results Sept 23, 2026.

items.lead: Lionsgate Studios is reportedly evaluating inbound takeover approaches — with France's Bollore Group and TV producer Banijay named as interested parties — sending shares up as much as 9% after hours.
items.why_unverified: Reuters 'sources' report; no deal is certain and Lionsgate could remain independent. Company has not confirmed, adviser unnamed, and Netflix earlier denied interest.

items.company: Thai Beverage / QSR of Asia (SGX: Y92)
items.lead: ThaiBev is reportedly considering a sale of QSR of Asia, Thailand's largest KFC franchise (500+ restaurants), and has enlisted Bank of America to gauge buyer interest amid a 21.7% profit decline.
items.why_unverified: Bloomberg 'said to' report; 'no sale is guaranteed at this time' and ThaiBev has not formally confirmed a process.

items.lead: Activist Harwood Capital, having built its stake above 8%, is publicly pushing for a piecemeal break-up of M&C Saatchi — selling its lobbying/events, advertising, and sports-marketing divisions — targeting a process within the next year.
items.why_unverified: Activist demand, not board-endorsed; M&C Saatchi declined to comment. No formal review or sale process launched and no adviser named.
